headline_rest: the way I was trained to read physical ones.
paragraphs: Physics taught me systems; software engineering taught me how products are actually built; years of language tutoring taught me how differently people think; copywriting taught me to be clear under commercial pressure. Delivery leadership is where all four converged — because most delivery failures live in the gap between what engineering means and what the business hears.

pillars.title: Systems thinker
pillars.description: I read an organization like a physical system — looking past the narrative for the hidden constraint. Teams rarely behave irrationally; systems often do.

pillars.title: Human translator
pillars.description: Engineers speak risk; product speaks outcomes; executives speak a third language. The highest-leverage act is usually interpretation — before any solution.

pillars.title: Three layers at once
pillars.description: I move fluently between executive strategy, flow analytics and tooling, and technical implementation — layers most leaders treat as separate.
